name: useAppendQueryParams
description: A hook for programmatically appending or updating URL query parameters with support for replace/push navigation and override modes.
usage_guidelines: Use for updating URL query parameters without full page reload.
Set replace: true to replace current history entry instead of pushing new one.
Set override: true to replace all query params instead of merging.
Empty, null, or undefined values automatically remove the parameter.
Useful for filters, pagination, and search state in URL.

onAppendQueryParams.type: (params: Record<string, unknown>, options?: {replace?: boolean, override?: boolean}) => void
onAppendQueryParams.description: Function to append/update query parameters. Options: replace (use replaceState), override (replace all params).

Do: Use for filter and pagination state
Use replace: true for filter updates
Use override: true when resetting filters
Remove params by setting to null/undefined
Dont: Don't store large objects in URL
Don't forget to parse params on mount
Don't use for temporary UI state
